events.title: Israel Launches Deadly Air Strikes on Gaza City
events.desc: At least seven Palestinians were killed in Gaza City when Israeli air strikes hit a residential building and a vehicle. Israel stated that the strikes targeted a Hamas member, described as one of the architects of the October 7 massacre.

events.title: Lebanon Says Six Killed in Israeli Strike as US Announces Ceasefire Extension
events.desc: Lebanon's Ministry of Health reported six people killed in an Israeli strike, amidst a US announcement extending the ceasefire. Israel has continued strikes, stating they are not subject to the truce. Hezbollah, which is not part of the ceasefire diplomacy, remains a factor in the ongoing conflict.

events.title: Israeli Strikes in Southern Lebanon Kill 22 People
events.desc: A series of Israeli air strikes in southern Lebanon resulted in the deaths of 22 people, including eight children, according to the Lebanese health ministry. The strikes hit areas including Nabatieh, where two paramedics were killed while on a rescue mission.

events.title: 'Floating Armoury' Ship Reportedly Seized by Iran
events.desc: A vessel described as a 'floating armoury' operating in the Gulf of Oman has reportedly been seized by Iranian military personnel. The incident raises concerns about maritime security in the region.

events.title: Iran War Live: Lebanon, Israel Extend Truce; Tehran Ready for More US Talks
events.desc: Lebanon and Israel have agreed to extend a ceasefire, with the US facilitating expanded talks for a political settlement. Despite this, Israel has conducted new strikes, asserting they are outside the truce's scope. The conflict continues to impact civilian populations.

events.title: US Says Iran-Backed Militia Commander Planned Jewish Site Attacks
events.desc: US authorities have detailed charges against Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi, a commander in Kataeb Hezbollah, an Iran-linked militia. He is accused of plotting attacks against Jewish targets in Europe and North America in retaliation for the war against Iran.

events.title: Trump says 20-year nuclear programme suspension by Iran would be enough
events.desc: US President Trump stated that Iran would need to suspend its nuclear program for 20 years and demonstrate a genuine commitment to removing nuclear fuel and halting uranium enrichment to satisfy US demands.

events.title: Israel Passes Law to Allow Death Penalty for Those Linked to October 7
events.desc: Israel has enacted a law permitting the death penalty and public trials for individuals involved in the Hamas-led attacks on October 7th. This legislation introduces a new punitive measure for those convicted of terrorism-related offenses.

events.title: Satellite Imagery Shows Ongoing Demolitions Across Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Despite a ceasefire, satellite imagery reveals extensive demolitions and destruction in southern Lebanese towns and villages within the 'Yellow Line' established by the IDF. Significant damage has been reported in at least 46 out of 54 surveyed locations.

events.title: US-Israel War Against Iran: Efforts to Destroy Police Infrastructure
events.desc: In the initial weeks of the conflict between the US/Israel and Iran, at least 80 police stations and associated infrastructure have been damaged or destroyed. Experts suggest this is part of a strategy to degrade the Iranian regime's 'repressive capacity'.

events.title: The War You’re Not Allowed to See: UAE Downplays Iranian Strikes
events.desc: Authorities in the United Arab Emirates have reportedly downplayed damage and mischaracterized interceptions related to Iranian drone strikes. This selective reporting contrasts with open-source evidence and suggests an effort to control the narrative surrounding the conflict.

events.title: Trump's China Summit Fails to Yield Breakthrough on Iran
events.desc: A high-profile summit between US President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping concluded without significant breakthroughs on the issue of Iran. While the visit involved extensive discussions, no new agreements or resolutions concerning Iran were announced.

events.title: Pro-Gaza Activist Seeks to Reopen Case Over Alleged Trump Administration Influence
events.desc: Lawyers for Mahmoud Khalil, a pro-Palestinian protest leader, are petitioning US immigration authorities to halt deportation proceedings. They allege the Trump administration 'secretly engineered' the outcome of his case, raising concerns about political influence on legal matters.

events.title: Iraqi Militia Leader Arrested in US Custody Linked to Pro-Iran Group
events.desc: An Iraqi militia leader, identified as Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi, has been taken into US custody. He is allegedly linked to a pro-Iran group suspected of orchestrating attacks against Jewish communities in Europe and North America.

events.title: Lebanon Extends Ceasefire with Israel Amidst Continued Strikes
events.desc: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend a ceasefire, with the US announcing expanded talks for a political settlement. However, Israel has continued to conduct strikes, asserting they are not subject to the truce, while Hezbollah, an Iran-backed group, remains active.
